title: Next demo run: tracing + retries to keep
summary: Keep claim-level trace summaries in the dashboard and retain full raw traces on disk; cap retries to a single automatic retry for transient errors and log any retry-related cost/latency impact. This preserves reviewer utility and demo responsiveness while staying within the approved cost boundary.

claims.text: Keeping more claim-level evidence increased median token usage (~+11%) but improved overall latency (~6%) because fewer outputs needed manual reruns; the latency gain came from fewer repeated attempts, not shorter single generations.

Finance will approve demo-quality runs up to the reference target cost budget if the report explains how extra tokens reduce review time; exceeding the budget requires reducing batch size, stricter retry caps, or more aggressive trace summarization.
Reviewer value comes from traces summarized next to the failed claim and cited source; raw provider payload dumps alone slowed review because reviewers had to search for relevant evidence.

For the live demo, single briefings felt responsive when they finished under ~10 seconds; stakeholders tolerated longer full runs if the UI continuously showed progress (current case, elapsed time) and artifact paths.

A demo-oriented retry policy should allow one automatic retry for transient provider errors, require a manifest entry when retries change cost/latency, and avoid silent repeated generations that make cost harder to explain.

openQuestions: Is the candidate prompt’s +11% token usage (with added trace retention) still within the reference target cost budget for the planned demo batch size?
Will the demo UI surface per-case progress and artifact paths continuously for full eval runs (to maintain tolerance for longer runs)?
Do we have the trace-summary format implemented (or can we mirror the mock) so summaries appear beside each failed claim during the demo?
recommendation: Use summarized, claim-attached tracing in the dashboard (keep full raw traces on disk) and cap retries at one automatic retry for transient errors, with manifest logging for any retry-driven cost/latency changes—provided the planned batch stays within the reference target cost budget or else reduce batch size / tighten retry caps per finance guidance.
